data.narrative:
Blackshark.ai is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Michael Putz that has raised $55M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 publicly traded peers in the Defense & Security sector, including same-stage companies Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression, using the company's $55M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yields $110M. Peer-Median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $94M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $205M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around which comparables best represent Blackshark.ai's positioning and growth trajectory within an active but heterogeneous defense-tech market. Stage-Progression produces the floor estimate, while Peer-Median skews higher, suggesting the peer set may reflect stronger earlier-stage capital efficiency or market positioning than the company currently commands. Key uncertainties include traction metrics relative to peers and the degree to which public-market multiples apply to this private cohort.
